steps.title: Use circle standard form
steps.content:
For a circle, the number on the right side of standard form is $r^2$, where $r$ is the radius.
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
The given equation has $144$ on the right side, so $r^2=144$. Take the positive square root because a radius is a distance.
$$r=\sqrt{144}=12$$
The answer is 12.
